case: The Zodiac Killer: the unidentified serial killer who terrorized Northern California and taunted police with cryptic letters
title: The Zodiac Killer: the unidentified serial killer who

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: And THIS is where it gets unhinged. You guys, I've been obsessed with the Zodiac Killer for like, ever. Since I was 12, I was hooked on true crime podcasts and books about this guy who's still out there, taunting us with his cryptic letters.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: Sorry WHAT? Like, how did you find him again?

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: Okay so picture this – you're in Northern California, it's the 60s, and suddenly these four people are dead. No one knows who did it, no one has any leads... it was like a real-life mystery novel.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: I've been thinking about him all day. Like, why do we still care so much?

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: Honestly? I think it's because he's just so... clever. He's always one step ahead of the police. And those letters! Oh my god, they're like a puzzle.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: I literally have no idea what his motives were. Like, was he trying to prove something? Was he just trying to freak everyone out?

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: Okay so here's the thing – the Zodiac Killer is actually considered one of the most prolific serial killers in American history. He killed at least 5 people, but some estimates say it could be up to 37.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: Wait wait wait... did you just say 37? Like, how did he even do that?

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: I know, right? It's like, how does someone get away with that for so long? Okay so picture this – the first murder was in December of '64. The police were baffled because there were no leads, no witnesses... it was like he just vanished into thin air.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: And THIS is where it gets unhinged. I mean, what if he's still out there?! What if he's watching us right now?!

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: Exactly, exactly! It's like, we can't even talk about this without getting a little freaked out. Okay so here's the thing – the police were able to catch him eventually, but it was like trying to find a needle in a haystack.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: I'm sorry — she did WHAT? Like, how did he manage that?

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: So the police found a bunch of clues, including one of his letters, and they were able to match it up with some handwriting samples. But here's the thing – even now, we don't know for sure who wrote those letters.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: Absolutely not. Like, can you even imagine being that person?!

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: I mean, I try to put myself in his shoes sometimes... like, what would drive someone to do something so heinous? But honestly, it's just too much for me.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: That's a solid 9 out of 10 on the unhinged scale because, let's be real, that guy is creepy. I mean, who writes letters to the police while they're investigating their own murders?!

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: Exactly! And what's even more messed up is that he was able to avoid detection for so long. It's like, how did we not catch on sooner?

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: But WHY would they... Like, why wouldn't someone just turn themselves in if they were going to get caught eventually?!

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: Okay so here's the thing – the police did finally catch up with him in 1970. He was arrested for suspicion of murder, but he never actually confessed or told anyone what really happened.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: It's like, we'll never know the truth now.

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: And that's what makes this case so fascinating to me – it's like, a real-life mystery novel that we can't solve. But at least we can explore it together, right?

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: Yeah... I guess you're right.

dialogue.speaker: MORGAN
dialogue.text: Okay everyone — thank you so much for listening to Dark Files. New episode drops [day of week]. Don't forget to subscribe, leave a review... and maybe don't go hiking alone.

dialogue.speaker: TAYLOR
dialogue.text: Stay curious, stay safe... and try not to think about the Zodiac Killer too much.
